content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Randall M. Ward 
Director 
Department of Conservation 
1416 Ninth street 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
Dear Randy: 
March 8, 1988 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-87-273 
I am sorry for my delay in responding to your letter of 
October 26, 1987. 
As you know, my mother was quite ill when we 
spoke, and I subsequently left the office for several months to 
care for her. 
Since your letter accurately summarizes our 
conversation, I did not rush to confirm it. 
Let me do so now. 
QUESTION 
If you exchange tickets to Sacramento Kings basketball 
games with a registered lobbyist, do you incur any reporting or 
disqualification duties? 
CONCLUSION 
You incur no reporting or disqualification duties if: 
1. 
You and the lobbyist exchange an equal number.of 
tickets at the same time, or 
2. 
You reimburse the lobbyist for the tickets within 30 
days of receipt. 
FACTS 
You purchased two season tickets to the Sacramento Kings 
basketball team. 
You sold one season ticket to a registered 
lobbyist for the amount you paid for it. 
You and the lobbyist attend games together. 
There are 
occasions when you cannot attend, and the lobbyist uses both 
tickets. 
When he cannot attend, you may use both tickets. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660 


Randall M. Ward 
March 8, 1988 
Page 2 
ANALYSIS 
The Political Reform Act (the "Act")Y defines "gift" as 
follows: 
(a) 
"Gift" means, except as provided in 
subdivision (b), any payment to the extent that 
consideration of equal or greater value is not 
received and includes a rebate or discount in the 
price of anything of value unless the rebate or 
discount is made in the regular course of business to 
members of the public without regard to official 
status .... 
(Section 82028(a).) 
Thus, if consideration of equal or greater value is 
the official, the official has not received a gift. 
if you and the lobbyist exchange an equal number of 
the same time, you have not received a gift. 
provided by 
Therefore, 
tickets at 
commission Regulation 18726.1(b) (4) provides that an 
official may, within 30 days of receipt, reimburse the donor 
for a gift. 
Under these circumstances, the official has not 
received a gift. 
Thus, if you and the lobbyist are not able to 
exchange an equal number of tickets at the same time, you may 
reimburse the lobbyist for the value of the tickets within 30 
days of receipt and incur no reporting or disqualification 
responsibilities. 
If you you have any questions concerning this letter, 
please give me a call at (916) 322-5901. 
DMG:plh 
Sincerely, 
O(~~ ~ h, d- (tjCi--" 
Diane M. Griffiths 
General Counsel 
Y 
Government Code sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations 
section 18000, et seg. 
All references to regulations are to Title 
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 


STATE OF CALIFORNIA-THE RESOURCES AGENCY 
GEORGE DEUKMEJIAN, Governor 
DEPARTMENT OF CONSERVATION 
DIVISION OF ADMINISTRATION 
DIVISION Of MINES AND GEOLOGY 
DIVISION OF Oil AND GAS 
DIVISION Of RECYCLING 
1416 Ninth Street 
SACRAMENTO, CA 
95814 
(916) 322-1080 
TOO (916) 324-2555 
Ms. Diane Griffiths 
General Counsel 
Fair Political Practices 
Commission 
P. O. Box 807 
Sacramento, CA 
95804 
Dear Ms. Griffiths: 
October 26, 1987 
As we discussed, I am planning to sell one of two season 
tickets to the Sacramento Kings Basketball Team to Mr. Ralph 
Simoni, a registered lobbyist with the firm of California 
Advocates. 
The sale price will be exactly what I paid for 
the ticket. 
We are planning to attend the games together; however, there 
will be occasions when I cannot attend and he will be using 
my ticket. 
When he cannot attend, I will be using his 
ticket. 
Based on your explanation, it is my understanding that as 
long as within a 3D-day period there is equal use of the 
tickets by both parties, or there is reimbursement for any 
tickets used, there is no conflict of interest pursuant to 
state law governing these matters. 
I appreciate very much your guidance. 
If you have any 
questions or require further information, please feel free to 
contact me. 
Rantlall M. Ward 
Director 
cc: 
Mr. John Larson, Chairman, Fair Political Practices 
Commission 
Mr. Ralph Simoni, California Advocates 


California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Randall M. Ward, Director 
Department of Conservation 
1416 Ninth street 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
Dear Mr. Ward: 
November 2, 1987 
Re: 
87-273 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform 
Act was received on October 30, 1987 by the Fair Political 
Practices commission. 
If you have any questions about your 
advice request, you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, 
or more information is needed, you should expect a response 
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written 
advice. 
If more information is needed, the person assigned to 
prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to 
advise you as to the information needed. 
If your request is 
for informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we 
can. 
(See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Adm. Code Sec. 
18329) .) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
JP:plh 
yours, 
eanne 
Chief 
V~~ 
Pritchard 
~~ 
Technical Assistance and Analysis 
Division 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 9C;R04~()R()7 •
